title: Lactuca oyukludaghensis
taxonomy: Lactuca oyukludaghensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the aster family. It is native to Turkey and was discovered in the Oyuklu Dağı mountains. Lactuca oyukludaghensis is classified as a part of the genus Lactuca, which includes several species of lettuce and related plants. The exact classification of Lactuca oyukludaghensis is as follows: Lactuca oyukludaghensis Turhanoglu & S. Ozkan. The plant's classification is further detailed in the APG III system.

intro: Lactuca oyukludaghensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), native to Turkey. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential in alleviating conditions such as arthritis, digestive problems, and infections. Bioactive constituents of Lactuca oyukludaghensis include sesquiterpenoids, lactones, and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lactuca oyukludaghensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support digestive health.
